title: verbascum subbiserratum
intro: Verbascum subbiserratum, also known as the biennial mullein, is a species of flowering plant in the mullein family, characterized by its large, hairy leaves and tall spikes of yellow flowers. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can be beneficial in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. The health benefits of Verbascum subbiserratum include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and act as a natural expectorant to help clear mucus from the lungs. The plant can be prepared for herbal use through infusions, decoctions, or topical applications, such as salves or ointments, to harness its medicinal properties.
